channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Designing disordered materials beyond equilibrium
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 26 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02552-3

A family of glassy materials emerges from local structural optimization, revealing an unexpected form of long-range orientational order.
item.dc:title: Designing disordered materials beyond equilibrium
item.dc:creator: Ludovic Berthier
M. D. Ediger

item.title: Entanglement made measurable
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 25 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02557-y

A resonant inelastic X-ray scattering interferometric approach exposes a highly entangled electronic ground state in a pyrochlore iridate, linking quantum entanglement to intertwined symmetry-breaking orders in a correlated metal near a metal–insulator transition.
item.dc:title: Entanglement made measurable
item.dc:creator: Riccardo Arpaia

item.title: A Floquet gap finally opens in graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02548-z

Time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy now resolves the long-sought avoided crossing in light-driven graphene, establishing a practical recipe for Floquet band engineering and sharpening prospects for light-induced topology in quantum materials.
item.dc:title: A Floquet gap finally opens in graphene
item.dc:creator: Michael A. Sentef
Marcel Reutzel

item.title: Observation of Floquet-induced gap in graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02549-y

Floquet engineering in monolayer graphene reveals a gap opening at Floquet band crossings and coherent Floquet sidebands resolved by time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy.
item.dc:title: Observation of Floquet-induced gap in graphene
item.dc:creator: Fei Wang
Xuanxi Cai
Xiao Tang
Jinxi Lu
Wanying Chen
Tianshuang Sheng
Runfa Feng
Haoyuan Zhong
Hongyun Zhang
Pu Yu
Shuyun Zhou

item.title: Perovskite–organic tandem solar cells with superior reverse-bias stability
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02541-6

Perovskite–organic-based solar cells typically show poor reverse-bias stability. By modulating the deep trap state in the bulk-heterojunction region, organic and perovskite–organic tandem solar cells show largely improved stability under reverse biases up to –40 V.
item.dc:title: Perovskite–organic tandem solar cells with superior reverse-bias stability
item.dc:creator: Jiaming Huang
Yu Han
Zhiwei Ren
Guang Yang
Yongmin Luo
Lei Cheng
Like Huang
Sudhi Mahadevan

Jinsong Huang
Gang Li

item.title: Three-dimensional photonic topological insulators go unbounded
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02559-w

Three-dimensional all-dielectric photonic topological insulators are experimentally shown to control far-field emission via the pseudo-spin degree of freedom.
item.dc:title: Three-dimensional photonic topological insulators go unbounded
item.dc:creator: Eran Lustig
